commit.message:
docs: add 10 security analysis features from copilot-cli-security (#218-#227)

Inspired by ivproduced/copilot-cli-security (ported from Gemini security extension):
- SAST scanner with 7 vuln categories
- Audit scope tool (git diff with ref validation)
- Line number finder for vulnerability locations
- Sandboxed PoC runner with timeout/extension restrictions
- Dependency scanner (OSV integration)
- Severity classifier (Critical/High/Medium/Low)
- PII flow tracer (source→sink analysis)
- LLM safety checker (prompt injection, unsafe output)
- Security report generator (markdown/JSON)
- Crypto weakness detector (DES/RC4/ECB/MD5/SHA1)

commit.message:
Add modern .NET 8 ADF SDK project with comprehensive activities

- Rewritten from .NET Framework 4.7.2 to .NET 8 with Azure.ResourceManager.DataFactory
- Azure.Identity (DefaultAzureCredential) replaces ADAL/MSAL
- Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets replaces Microsoft.Azure.KeyVault
- Activities: Copy, Web, Lookup, ForEach, If Condition, Switch, Until, Wait,
  Set/Append Variable, Stored Procedure, Delete, Validation, Execute Pipeline
- Triggers: Schedule, Tumbling Window, Blob Event, Custom Event
- Monitoring: Pipeline runs, activity runs, trigger runs, rerun failed
- Integration Runtime: Azure IR, Self-Hosted IR
- Full async/await with CancellationToken throughout
- Retry logic for transient failures
- Open-source APIs (jsonplaceholder, httpbin) for Web Activity demos

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: ADF-DOT-Net
repository.description: End-to-end Azure Data Factory management using C# .NET 8 SDK — Create pipelines, linked services, datasets, triggers, activities (Copy, Web, Lookup, ForEach, If Condition, Switch, Until), monitoring, and CI/CD with Azure.ResourceManager.DataFactory

commit.message:
Security & resilience: XSS prevention, retry logic, rate limiting, accessibility

Phase 1 production hardening:
- Add DOMPurify for HTML sanitization (XSS prevention on import/signature)
- Wrap all JSON.parse in safe try/catch with fallback defaults (6 locations)
- Add retry with exponential backoff to graphFetch (500/502/timeout/429)
- Add 30s request timeout on all Graph API calls
- Add real rate limiting engine (token bucket, 30/min enforced)
- Add send progress checkpointing to localStorage
- Respect Retry-After header from 429 responses
- Escape showStatus messages to prevent XSS
- Remove inline onclick handler (CSP compliance)
- Add ARIA roles/labels to all 7 modal dialogs
- Add role=alert, aria-live to results container
- Add role=textbox, aria-multiline to rich editor
- Add type=email to CC/BCC/unsubscribe inputs
- Add required attribute to subject input
- Add .gitignore, LICENSE (MIT), CHANGELOG.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: annotate readFile path as validated (CodeQL false positive)

The path is sanitized via safeRealpath (symlink resolution) + isUnderHome
(homedir jail) + extension allowlist before reaching readFile. CodeQL
cannot trace taint through the cross-module sanitizer in path-utils.ts.

fix(api): use discordsays redirect URI for Activity token exchange

Discord embedded SDK authorize() always uses
https://<CLIENT_ID>.discordsays.com as the redirect URI.
The code exchange must pass the same URI or Discord returns
redirect_uri_mismatch.

- Default redirectUri for /auth/discord/activity/token is now
  https://<clientId>.discordsays.com (built from configured client ID)
- isAllowedActivityRedirectUri now accepts *.discordsays.com
- Client-supplied redirectUri override still validated + accepted

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(provider): disable cookie quoting for Yandex compatibility

aiohttp defaults to quote_cookie=True which percent-encodes special
characters in cookie values. Yandex services reject quoted cookies,
causing persistent 401 errors. Set quote_cookie=False to match
AlexxIT's original YandexStation implementation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: accept any unix socket in checkDockerHost (#1924)

The checkDockerHost function used a Set of two hardcoded socket paths
(/var/run/docker.sock, /run/docker.sock) to validate DOCKER_HOST. Any
other unix socket path (e.g. /tmp/custom-docker.sock) was incorrectly
rejected as an external daemon.

All unix:// sockets are local by definition — only TCP endpoints
(tcp://host:port) indicate an external Docker daemon incompatible
with AWF's network isolation model.

Replace the Set lookup with a unix:// prefix check.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: apply 25 findings from 6-model hardcore review (iteration 1)

Security fixes:
- Plugin execute_sync: capability check + env var filtering (LD_PRELOAD etc) + 5s timeout
- AgentIdentity: rename public_key→verification_hash, add non-crypto warnings
- SSH Password: redacted Debug impl, plaintext warning docs
- BridgeConfig: default to wss:// (TLS), add tls field
- validate_memory_source: rename Verified→HasSourceMarkers, document heuristic

Correctness fixes:
- UTF-8 panic in compaction preview (byte→char slicing)
- path_ids cycle detection (HashSet visited guard)
- PatternCompactor retention window off-by-one
- MessageBus::read_since subscription enforcement
- OpLog::merge dedup includes op_type
- record_write only resets entrenchment on Allow
- PatternAggregator duplicate session counting (HashSet)
- SkillEvolver uses all patterns (not just first)
- BranchReflector path parsing for --> prefix

Performance fixes:
- ContextRotDetector: Vec→VecDeque for O(1) pop_front
- MemoryGarbageCollector: Vec::contains→HashSet
- Bounded telemetry histories (100 max)

React fixes:
- DesignMode: stageRef for correct drag math, listener cleanup, draft pruning
- VoiceInput: disabled guard, buffer reuse
- ImageRenderer: SSR guard, lazy/async/no-referrer
- BuddySprite: conditional tabIndex

Tests: 838 → 853 (15 new regression tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: apply pathPrefix to root-relative URLs for GitHub Pages deployment (#26)

* fix: apply pathPrefix to root-relative URLs in HTML output

Add an Eleventy HTML transform that rewrites all root-relative href/src
attributes with the configured pathPrefix at build time. This fixes the
April preview deployment to GitHub Pages where assets (CSS, JS, images)
and page links were missing the /praxis/ prefix.

The transform is a no-op when pathPrefix is '/' (Vercel/local builds)
and prevents double-prefixing of already-prefixed URLs.

* fix: handle query strings and fragments in double-prefix prevention

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/verboEse/praxis/sessions/318b95ed-b465-4ec3-93d3-5d3a1ee6c41f

commit.message:
Add weekly auto-refresh workflow for maintainers (#62)

* Add weekly auto-refresh workflow for maintainers

Wire Update-Maintainers.ps1 into a weekly cron (Monday 03:37 UTC).

Safety guardrails:
- Additive only: unions discovered maintainers with existing list
- Preserves repos in maintainers.json not yet in repos.json
- Aborts if total maintainer count would decrease
- Aborts if any existing repo key would be lost
- Push retry with rebase (3 attempts)
- Per-repo API failures keep existing data unchanged

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Adds comprehensive technical specifications for:

- #188: Content management system for experiences, specialists, venues, tiers

Co-authored-by: ivegamsft <ivegamsft@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Reduce publication benchmark settings for faster execution (#157)

* Reduce publication benchmark settings for faster execution

Change default (publication-quality) JMH settings from:
  -f 5 -wi 5 -w 10 -i 5 -r 10  (~500s/method, ~35h total)
to:
  -f 3 -wi 3 -w 5 -i 5 -r 5   (~120s/method, ~8.5h total)

3 forks with 15 samples per method is still rigorous enough for
meaningful confidence intervals. The biggest savings come from
reducing warmup (pure dead time) from 50s to 15s per fork.

Fixes #156

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix BENCHMARKS.md to reflect actual JMH settings used

The data in BENCHMARKS.md was collected with annotation-driven settings
(1 fork, 3 warmup × 1s, 5 measurement × 1s) before #152 discovered
the mismatch. Update the methodology section to be honest about what
settings were actually used, and note the planned re-run.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Copilot Readiness Checker — free tool #8

Interactive 30-question assessment scoring M365 Copilot readiness
across 7 pillars (licensing, identity, data governance, security,
network, change management, monitoring). Features animated score ring,
per-pillar breakdown bars, actionable recommendations with Learn docs
links, shareable URLs, print-friendly view, keyboard navigation.
100% client-side JS, zero API calls.

- Blue #3B82F6 accent, readiness.css + readiness.js
- Added to nav dropdown, free-tools page, baseof body class
- Cross-links to Licensing Simplifier, M365 Roadmap, Service Health

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add blob API round-trip, fix cache logging, add version endpoint

- POST/GET/DELETE /api/blobs/* for app-level blob access
- GET /api/blobs/roundtrip for automated write/read/verify/delete test
- GET /api/version for environment isolation proof
- Add cache set/get logging to debug cache behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: production hardening — rate limiting, structured logging, error handlers, TLS

- Rate limiting: slowapi on all auth endpoints (10/min login, 30/min /me, 5/min account delete)
- Structured logging: JSON formatter via python-json-logger, log auth events, room lifecycle, errors
- Global error handler: catch-all returns generic 500, logs real traceback server-side
- Validation error handler: sanitized 422 responses without pydantic internals
- Security headers: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy, HSTS
- CORS restricted: explicit methods (GET/POST/DELETE/OPTIONS) and headers instead of wildcards
- TLS infrastructure: self-signed cert generator, nginx-ssl.conf, docker-compose.prod.yml, livekit-prod.yaml
- Debug mode: docs/redoc hidden when DEBUG=false, HSTS only in production
- 12 new tests covering all hardening features (50 total backend tests)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: fepegar <12688084+fepegar@users.noreply.github.com>

perf: fix DebugHost /engine startup from 6s to sub-1ms (#77)

* perf: fix DebugHost /engine startup from 6s to sub-1ms (#76)

Root cause: DebugHost defaulted to http://localhost:50051 while the Rust
engine binds 127.0.0.1:50051. On Windows, localhost resolves to ::1 first,
adding ~2s per gRPC channel. The /engine page created 3 channels
sequentially, paying the penalty 3x.

Changes:

Layer 1 - Fix default endpoint:
- Change DefaultEngineEndpoint from localhost to 127.0.0.1 in
  DebugWorkbenchOptions, appsettings.json, appsettings.Development.json,
  Engine.razor guidance text, and CliArgumentParser.

Layer 2 - Reuse GrpcChannel per endpoint:
- Convert GrpcClientFactory to cache a single GrpcChannel per endpoint
  lifetime with thread-safe invalidation on endpoint change.
- Configure SocketsHttpHandler with EnableMultipleHttp2Connections,
  KeepAlivePingDelay (60s), PooledConnectionIdleTimeout (5m).
- Remove channel-per-operation dispose pattern from all 5 workbench
  services (SetupWorkbenchService, StationProfileWorkbenchService,
  RuntimeConfigWorkbenchService, LookupWorkbenchService,
  StorageWorkbenchService).

Layer 3 - Parallel startup RPCs:
- Change Engine.razor OnInitializedAsync from sequential awaits to
  Task.WhenAll for the 3 independent refresh calls.

Layer 5 - Cache ToolchainLocator results:
- Cache PATH scan results in singleton on first call.
- Fix GetToolAvailability calling each Find method twice per tool.

Benchmarks - Add regression harness:
- Add BenchmarkDotNet project with 9 scenarios across localhost/127.0.0.1.
- New scenarios: warm channel reuse, tuned SocketsHttpHandler, cold
  channel isolation, parallel on warm channel.

BenchmarkDotNet results (warm channel, parallel RPCs):
  Before: 6,079 ms (3 channels, sequential, localhost)
  After:    0.88 ms (warm channel, parallel, either host)
  Improvement: 6,907x

Named pipes evaluated and deferred: warm channel parallel is already
sub-millisecond on TCP. Named pipes would add dual-transport complexity
for ~0.5ms theoretical gain.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: use double-checked locking in GrpcClientFactory

Replace lock-on-every-call with volatile CachedChannel record and
double-checked locking. The lock is now only taken on the cold path
(first call or endpoint change), avoiding contention on the hot path.

Addresses review feedback from Treit on PR #77.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Mark default version as not-for-publishing and ContinuationMerger as experimental

- Change default Version from 0.1.0 to 0.1.0-notforpublishing so builds
  from source are never mistaken for real releases (actual versions come
  from tags).
- Add [Experimental("STDF0001")] to ContinuationMerger since it drops
  TrailingData and breaks byte-exact round-trip fidelity.

commit.message:
feat(dashboard): Sprint 6 — 10 dashboard improvements with QA fixes

Changes:
- Move Active/Used badge to period banner (smaller, status-aware)
- Shrink benefit card height (min-h 200→160, reduced padding)
- Move delete to edit modal; add claimedAt date picker + Mark Unused
- Annual benefits show 'Mark Used' instead of 'Mark Jan Used'
- Progress ring always shows %, yearly progress via calculateYearlyUsage()
- Fix spacing gap after cards (py-8→py-6)
- Total benefits sums across ALL cards with deduplication
- Period filters reordered: All Time → Year → Half-year → Quarter → Month
- 13 benefit category icons (insurance, entertainment, streaming, etc.)

QA fixes (3 rounds):
- Fix data-loss: merge partial API responses instead of full replace
- Fix status filter: isUsed checked before apiStatus for correct filtering
- Fix stale closures: all setBenefits calls use functional updater pattern
- Add server-side claimedAt validation in PATCH route
- Fix null clearing for userDeclaredValue, claimedAt, expirationDate
- Fix userDeclaredValue=0 display (use != null, not truthiness)
- Fix expiration date validation (compare date-only, not datetime)
- Fix unit mismatch in new dashboard (cents→dollars conversion)
- Remove dead code: unused onDelete prop, _usage param, aria-disabled
- Remove role=status from period banner (prevents excessive SR announcements)
- Fix date picker helper text for partial period bounds

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor, performance, grid, tests, and content pack reorganisation

- Remove ground plane: delete StaircaseHole.tsx, strip all groundPlane/
  showGroundPlane state, actions, serialization fields, and UI (MoveToolPanel)
- Restore staircase floor-tile suppression without ground plane dependency
  (blockedFloorCellKeys computed directly in DungeonRoom)
- Reorganise core content pack into tiles/, props/, openings/ subdirectories;
  update all relative import paths
- Fix flipped flag missing from opening serialization (SerializedOpening now
  includes flipped; read back in parseFloorData)
- Unified grid system: replace gridHelper + per-cell FloorGridOverlay instanced
  mesh with two-layer TSL approach — full-coverage base plane (dark lines +
  thick X=0/Z=0 axis lines) plus per-cell instanced glow layer so cursor reveal
  only appears over floor tiles, not void
- Tune grid opacity: glow alpha 0.42→0.18, axis alpha 0.42→0.28, colours muted
- Add AGENTS.md with stack, commands, architecture, and commit conventions
- Add FpsCounter component (lazy-loaded)
- Expand test coverage: new serialization.test.ts (12 tests covering roundtrip,
  error cases, v1→v5 migrations); extend useDungeonStore.test.ts with 17 new
  tests for openings, floors, ensureAdjacentFloor, newDungeon, getOpeningSegments

commit.message:
fix: send track_id as query param, not HTTP header (#13)

/auth/session/ expects track_id as a URL query parameter (?track_id=...),
not as an HTTP header. With a header the endpoint ignores the track and
returns generic cookies without auth context — Quasar IoT returns 401.

Closes #13

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
